chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يُغَسِّلُ الْمَرْأَةَ وَ الْمَرْأَةِ تُغَسِّلُ الرَّجُلَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 29 – The man washes the woman, and the woman washes the man

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/337)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/337)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَخْرُجُ فِي السَّفَرِ وَ مَعَهُ امْرَأَتُهُ يُغَسِّلُهَا قَالَ نَعَمْ وَ أُمُّهُ وَ أُخْتُهُ وَ نَحْوُ هَذَا يُلْقَى عَلَى عَوْرَتِهَا خِرْقَةٌ .

en.hubeali: Abu Ali Al Ashary, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar and Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Fazl Bin Shazaan, altogether from Safwan Bin Yahya, from Mansour Bin Hazim who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about the man going out in a journey and with him is his wife, can he wash her (if she were to die)?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes, and (he can wash) his mother, and his sister and such as this, casting a cloth upon her private part’.
en.sarwar: 8. Abu Ali al-Ash’ariy has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd al-Jabbar and Muhammad ibn ’Isma‘il has narrated from al-Fadl ibn Shadhan from all from Safwan ibn Yahya from Mansur ibn Hazim who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who goes on a journey with his wife (and she dies), can he give her Ghusl (bath)? He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, he can do so and so also is the case of his mother and sister. Such person’s private parts are covered with a piece of cloth.’”
